paragraphs.text: Im here to share my opinion why the Digital Fairness Act should include some sort of clause to prevent Game Publishers from making a game unplayable after certain circumstances, whether the servers for online games have shut down or support is discontiniued, many games have been made completely unplayable for the customer this way. We are talking about works of art here, crafted from numerous hard working game developers and artists that clock hours upon hours to tell a story for other people to enjoy and get immersed in. While some games thrive from such storys, like the Assassins Creed series or the award winning game Red Dead Redemption 2, others profit from their unique gameplay and teamplay, simillar to sports. People dont only play these games, they get immersed in those worlds and sometimes even make friends online through them. This alone should be reason why such game should never be left in an unplayable state. On top of this people have bought and payed for these kinds of games, but only for a license. Aswell as other monetized content like customizations. This practice is not fair towards the customer as there is no other way to obtain acsess to such a game. There have been several cases where i bought acsess to games only for them to shut down servers a couple of weeks later, with no chance for me to really experience what i paid for. So my suggestion would be one or multiple of the following solutions: -set a clause for the playability of Singleplayer and Multiplayer games. Where an full functioning offline version would be downloaded together with the main game, so customers keep acsess even though support has been discontiniued or servers have been shut down. -Set a clause to have multiple purchase options other than only purchasing a license. -Set a clause that prohibits the common neccessity to logg in with an App (EA Play, Ubisoft connect for example) in order to acsess a game, instead games must be accsessable from the customers console or computer directly without extra steps. Adding clauses focused on the consumer rights around video games would resonate with millions of customers internationally and would help saving memories and experiences that only Art can create. I appreciate the time and effort for this Initiative and thanks to all lawmakers, contributors and team members behind this Digital Fairness Act for their invaluable work.
